goal: Audit every feature, derive canonical user stories, track status in one spreadsheet, then test and fix all user stories with TDD and retesting.

rows.title: Help and version entrypoints
rows.user_story: As a user, I can ask for help or the installed version without supplying media input.
rows.expected_behavior: --help/-h prints usage text and exits 0; --version/-V prints `fscript <version>` and exits 0 before normal parsing.
rows.notes: Short-circuit precedence over invalid trailing args is code-defined but not explicitly smoke-tested.

rows.title: Default invocation contract
rows.user_story: As a user, `fscript <media-or-url>` should choose a sensible default pipeline and output mode.
rows.expected_behavior: Defaults are speakers+timestamps output, cleaning on, chunk 120, overlap 2, no explicit path, and coreml diarization only when the helper is available; otherwise diarization is disabled with a notice.
rows.notes: Automated tests passed and the README now states that speaker-aware text is the default on-disk output.

rows.title: Output destination selection
rows.user_story: As a user, I can write results to a default file, explicit file, directory, or stdout.
rows.expected_behavior: Destination may be omitted, positional, `-o/--output`, `--stdout`, or positional `-`; file mode prints only the absolute output path on stdout and status on stderr.
rows.notes: Smoke coverage already proved normal destination modes, and the parser now rejects `--output` followed by another flag instead of writing to a file literally named after that flag.

rows.title: Output path conflict guards
rows.user_story: As a user, I should get a clear error instead of ambiguous output routing.
rows.expected_behavior: --stdout cannot be combined with `--output` or a positional destination; positional destination cannot be combined with `--output`; extra positional args error.
rows.notes: Messages vary slightly by path but enforce the same contract.

rows.title: Hyphen-prefixed argument safety
rows.user_story: As a user, an unknown short flag should fail instead of silently becoming a filename.
rows.expected_behavior: Unknown `-X` args error; positional `-` means stdout; real paths starting with `-` must be prefixed with `./`.
rows.notes: Prevents accidental creation of files like `-Z`.

rows.title: Output mode surface and exclusivity
rows.user_story: As a user, I can choose exactly one transcript format.
rows.expected_behavior: Supported modes are `--json`, `--speakers[=plain|timestamps]`, `--text[=plain|compact|timestamps]`, `--srt`, and `--vtt`; they are mutually exclusive; default is speakers with timestamps.
rows.notes: Verified by new unit and CLI smoke tests: invalid spaced values after `--speakers` or `--text` now error instead of becoming positional output paths.

rows.title: Speaker-aware text rendering
rows.user_story: As a user, I can get speaker-labeled output with or without timestamps.
rows.expected_behavior: Speaker labels normalize like `S1` -> `SPEAKER_01`; consecutive same-speaker segments merge; timestamped mode prefixes `HH:MM:SS -`; plain mode omits timestamps; missing speaker labels do not emit `UNKNOWN:`.
rows.notes: If `segments` is absent, speaker output renders as an empty string.

rows.title: Plain, compact, and timestamped text modes
rows.user_story: As a user, I can request transcript text without speaker formatting.
rows.expected_behavior: `--text` writes one timestamped line per non-empty segment; `--text=plain` writes one trimmed line per segment and falls back to `result.text` when no segments exist; `--text=compact` flattens `result.text` into one line.
rows.notes: Timestamped text with no segments renders empty output.

rows.title: Text modes disable diarization
rows.user_story: As a user, text-only output should not trigger speaker diarization.
rows.expected_behavior: Any `--text...` mode clears diarization settings, ignores diarization flags, and warns `--text output ignores diarization flags; continuing without diarization.` when those flags were supplied.
rows.notes: Missing-helper warnings are intentionally suppressed in text mode.

rows.title: JSON output schema
rows.user_story: As a script consumer, I can parse a stable machine-readable result object.
rows.expected_behavior: `--json` pretty-prints `BenchmarkResult`; field names are snake_case; optional `segments`, per-segment `speaker`, and `speaker_diarization` are omitted when absent; cleaning applies before serialization when enabled.
rows.notes: Tests assert some fields but do not lock the full schema.

rows.title: Experimental SRT and VTT rendering
rows.user_story: As a user, I can export subtitles from transcript segments.
rows.expected_behavior: `--srt` renders numbered cues with `HH:MM:SS,mmm`; `--vtt` renders `WEBVTT` plus `HH:MM:SS.mmm` cues; both skip empty or non-positive-duration segments; speaker labels are prefixed when present.
rows.notes: VTT with no valid cues becomes just `WEBVTT`; empty-SRT behavior is code-defined but lightly tested.

rows.title: Cleaning toggle and repeated-word collapse
rows.user_story: As a user, I get conservative cleanup by default and can opt out.
rows.expected_behavior: Cleaning is on by default; `--raw` disables it; `--clean` re-enables it; `--raw` and `--clean` conflict; cleanup only collapses sufficiently long pathological repeated runs.
rows.notes: Ordinary repetition and longer words are intentionally left alone.

rows.title: Diarization flag selection and validation
rows.user_story: As a user, I can enable, disable, or tune diarization explicitly.
rows.expected_behavior: Supports `-d/--diarize [coreml|lseend-dihard3]`, `-D/--no-diarization`, and legacy `--backend`; bare `--diarize` means coreml; lseend defaults threshold to 0.3; `--num-speakers` is coreml-only; conflicting selections error.
rows.notes: `--diarize none` is still rejected in favor of `-D`, and invalid spaced values after bare `--diarize` now error instead of being reinterpreted as positional output paths.

rows.title: Chunk, model, and remote override flags
rows.user_story: As a user, I can override chunking, model locations, and remote-local routing from the CLI.
rows.expected_behavior: `--chunk/--chunk-seconds`, `--overlap/--chunk-overlap-seconds`, `--model-dir`, `--model-package`, `--model-url`, `-l`, `--local`, and `--prefer-local-for-remote` all update parser state and surface in execution.
rows.notes: Happy-path smoke coverage already exercised chunk/model/remote overrides, and new unit+smoke tests now prove that `--model-dir`, `--model-package`, and `--model-url` reject a following flag token as a missing value.

rows.title: Packaged binary help/version parity
rows.user_story: As a user installing via wheel or release artifact, I should see the same basic CLI surface.
rows.expected_behavior: The bundled wheel binary must match the release binary for `--help` and `--version`; the PyPI wrapper forwards argv to the bundled binary and returns its exit code.
rows.notes: Local pytest now skips this check when no wheel has been built, and after generating `dist-pypi/fscript-1.1.3-*.whl` the parity test passes end to end.

rows.title: Model bootstrap and cache reuse
rows.user_story: As a user, the model should appear automatically and stay reusable across runs.
rows.expected_behavior: The app requires four model files; if they are missing it downloads the tarball if needed, otherwise reuses the cached package, extracts it, removes AppleDouble files, optionally renames the extracted default directory into a custom target, and errors if the final model dir is still incomplete.
rows.notes: No checksum/version validation; a bad cached tarball is reused before failing.

rows.title: Audio suitability check and normalization
rows.user_story: As a user, media should be normalized only when the input audio is not already in the supported format.
rows.expected_behavior: `ffprobe` inspects the first audio stream; the app accepts audio as-is only when it is 16 kHz mono PCM16; otherwise `ffmpeg` writes a temp `<stem>.16k_mono.wav` and marks the run as normalized.
rows.notes: Missing `ffprobe` or `ffmpeg` hard-fails the run.

rows.title: Local ASR benchmark result generation
rows.user_story: As a user, a local transcription run should return transcript content plus timing metadata.
rows.expected_behavior: Local input flows into `transcribe_audio_input`, which ensures the model, normalizes audio, reads WAV samples, loads the int8 Parakeet ONNX model, transcribes with segment timestamps, optionally diarizes, and returns `BenchmarkResult` metadata.
rows.notes: Zero-length audio would risk `inf`/`NaN` timing ratios.

rows.title: Default chunking with tunable overlap and disable switch
rows.user_story: As a user transcribing long audio, I want chunked inference by default and a way to tune or disable it.
rows.expected_behavior: Defaults are chunk 120 and overlap 2; `--chunk 0` disables chunking and forces overlap 0; otherwise overlap must be smaller than chunk size and both values are rounded to samples.
rows.notes: Sample rounding can shift exact chunk boundaries slightly.

rows.title: Chunk boundary text deduplication
rows.user_story: As a user, overlapped chunks should merge into one coherent transcript instead of repeating boundary words.
rows.expected_behavior: Chunk texts are merged by case-insensitive normalized word overlap up to 64 words; a one-word overlap is ignored when the left side already ends with sentence punctuation.
rows.notes: Dedup depends on normalization that strips non-alphanumeric edges.

rows.title: Transcript segment fallback and merge behavior
rows.user_story: As a user, I want segment timestamps when available and a fallback segment when the model does not return segments.
rows.expected_behavior: Model segments become transcript segments when present and valid; otherwise the code creates a single fallback segment spanning the chunk; overlapping adjacent segments are merged when text is duplicated or dedup improves on raw concatenation.
rows.notes: Only temporally overlapping segments are candidates for merge.

rows.title: Optional diarization attachment after ASR
rows.user_story: As a user, speaker labeling should happen after transcription when diarization is active.
rows.expected_behavior: After ASR, `maybe_diarize_segments` either returns segments unchanged when no request exists or merges diarization output into transcript segments and attaches backend metadata.
rows.notes: Kept as pipeline behavior because it changes the post-ASR data shape.

rows.title: TTY-aware progress reporting
rows.user_story: As a user, long chunked runs should show lightweight progress instead of silent waiting.
rows.expected_behavior: Chunked transcription starts `ChunkProgressReporter`; interactive stderr gets a spinner plus fixed-width progress bar, while non-TTY stderr gets coarse progress lines and a final completion line.
rows.notes: Formatting is tested; thread and join behavior are not.

rows.title: Local vs remote input detection
rows.user_story: As a user, URLs should be treated as remote media and everything else as a local path.
rows.expected_behavior: Inputs starting with `http://` or `https://` become `RemoteUrl`; other values become `LocalPath` without additional heuristics.
rows.notes: Non-HTTP remote schemes are not supported.

rows.title: yt-dlp command discovery with uvx fallback
rows.user_story: As a user, remote features should still work when `yt-dlp` is available via `uvx` instead of PATH.
rows.expected_behavior: The app first checks `yt-dlp --version`; if that fails it checks `uvx yt-dlp --version`; if that succeeds, later commands run through `uvx yt-dlp`; otherwise it falls back to plain `yt-dlp` and lets execution fail clearly.
rows.notes: Important repo contract, currently untested in-code.

rows.title: Remote metadata inspection
rows.user_story: As a user, the app should inspect remote media before deciding whether subtitles or audio download are needed.
rows.expected_behavior: `load_remote_video_info` runs `yt-dlp --dump-single-json --no-playlist`, parses the JSON metadata, and surfaces install guidance when the command is unavailable.
rows.notes: Verified in an end-to-end run on 2026-07-01 with the README sample YouTube URL; failure paths are still not unit-tested.

rows.title: Manual subtitle language preference
rows.user_story: As a user, when manual subtitles exist the app should prefer the most relevant language automatically.
rows.expected_behavior: Language preference order is `pt-BR`, `pt`, `en`, `en-US`, then the first other language with tracks.
rows.notes: This is only based on manual subtitles; auto-captions are not used for the shortcut path.

rows.title: Manual subtitle format choice
rows.user_story: As a user, the app should choose a supported subtitle format automatically.
rows.expected_behavior: If any track for the chosen language is `vtt`, the app requests VTT; otherwise it requests `json3`.
rows.notes: Selection logic is implemented but not unit-tested directly.

rows.title: VTT subtitle parsing
rows.user_story: As a user, manual VTT subtitles should become timed transcript segments.
rows.expected_behavior: The parser normalizes line endings, skips headers, finds `-->` timing lines, collapses cue text whitespace, ignores empty or non-positive cues, and returns timed segments.
rows.notes: Style metadata on timing lines is ignored after the end timestamp.

rows.title: json3 subtitle parsing
rows.user_story: As a user, YouTube json3 subtitles should become timed transcript segments.
rows.expected_behavior: The parser reads `tStartMs`, `dDurationMs`, and nested `segs[].utf8`, filters blank pieces, collapses whitespace, and returns timed segments with no speaker labels.
rows.notes: Malformed payloads return a parsing error.

rows.title: Manual subtitle shortcut path
rows.user_story: As a user, when clean manual subtitles exist I want the app to use them instead of running local ASR.
rows.expected_behavior: For remote input, if `--local` is not forced and diarization is not active, the app tries to download manual subtitles; when non-empty subtitle text exists it returns a `remote-manual-subtitle` benchmark result and writes output without local transcription.
rows.notes: Times, speed, and local-model markers are zeroed in the manual subtitle result; README now matches the narrower condition that this path only applies when diarization is inactive and `--local` is not set.

rows.title: Remote audio download fallback
rows.user_story: As a user, remote URLs should still be transcribed locally when subtitle shortcutting is not used.
rows.expected_behavior: The app downloads best audio via `yt-dlp`, captures the final moved filepath from stdout, then sends that audio through the normal local transcription pipeline.
rows.notes: Remote alias smoke tests cover the forced-local flags, and a full run on 2026-07-01 against the README sample YouTube URL completed with `transcript_source = downloaded-audio-local-model`.

rows.title: Configured diarization binary detection
rows.user_story: As a user, diarization should honor `FSCRIPT_DIARIZATION_BINARY` and classify missing helpers accurately.
rows.expected_behavior: When the configured binary is executable, status is `Available`; when env points to a bare command missing from PATH it is `MissingConfiguredBinaryOnPath`; when env points to a non-executable path it is `InvalidConfiguredBinaryPath`; without env and no helper on PATH it is `MissingDefaultBinary`.
rows.notes: Repo instructions explicitly warn not to rely only on `which` when reasoning about this binary.

rows.title: Missing-helper fallback notices
rows.user_story: As a user, I should get a clear explanation when diarization is unavailable.
rows.expected_behavior: Fallback notices differ for missing default helper, missing configured PATH command, and invalid configured path, and explain whether the app is continuing with plain transcription.
rows.notes: Default-mode parsing surfaces these notices instead of hard-failing.

rows.title: Backend command construction and failure handling
rows.user_story: As a user, explicit diarization should call the right backend with the right options and fail clearly when the backend is missing.
rows.expected_behavior: CoreML runs `fluidaudiocli process --mode offline`; lseend runs `fluidaudiocli lseend --variant dihard3`; threshold and num-speakers are appended when supplied; missing executables raise a clear error naming the requested backend.
rows.notes: Smoke tests verify the arguments written to a fake helper.

rows.title: Diarization output normalization and speaker merge
rows.user_story: As a user, backend diarization output should map cleanly onto transcript segments.
rows.expected_behavior: The parser accepts both `speakerId` and `speaker`, drops empty or non-positive segments, then assigns each transcript segment the speaker with the largest temporal overlap and records metadata about backend name and segment counts.
rows.notes: lseend payloads may omit speaker_count and still parse successfully.

rows.title: No-speech diarization failure fallback
rows.user_story: As a user, diarization should not fail the whole transcription when the backend only reports that no speech was found.
rows.expected_behavior: If the diarization error text matches `nospeechdetected` or `no speech detected`, the app logs `speaker diarization skipped: ...` and returns plain transcript segments without metadata; other diarization errors still fail.
rows.notes: This is a targeted resilience escape hatch, not a general suppress-all-errors rule.

rows.title: Render diarized transcript into screenplay-style text
rows.user_story: As a user with transcript JSON on disk, I can generate a speaker-prefixed script text file without rerunning transcription.
rows.expected_behavior: `render_diarized_transcript_script.py` reads `segments`, skips blank or invalid entries, normalizes simple speaker IDs, merges consecutive turns by default, writes `<input>.script.txt` unless an explicit output path is passed, creates parent directories, and prints the final path.
rows.notes: Unlike the main Rust output path, missing speakers become the configured unknown label instead of silently omitting a prefix.
